info.name: Mark
info.desc: The Holy Gospel According to Mark

1:
The beginning of the good news about Jesus the Messiah,
 the Son of God,
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
1:1 Some manuscripts do not have the Son of God.; 1:1 Or Jesus Christ. Messiah (Hebrew) and Christ (Greek) both mean Anointed One.
2:
as it is written in Isaiah the prophet:
“I will send my messenger ahead of you,
who will prepare your way”
—
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
3:
“a voice of one calling in the wilderness,
‘Prepare the way for the Lord,
make straight paths for him.’ ”
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
; 1:3 Isaiah 40:3
4: And so John the Baptist appeared in the wilderness, preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
5: The whole Judean countryside and all the people of Jerusalem went out to him. Confessing their sins, they were baptized by him in the Jordan River.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
6: John wore clothing made of camel’s hair, with a leather belt around his waist, and he ate locusts and wild honey.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
7: And this was his message: “After me comes the one more powerful than I, the straps of whose sandals I am not worthy to stoop down and untie.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
8:
I baptize you with
 water, but he will baptize you with
 the Holy Spirit.”
The Baptism and Testing of Jesus
1:8 Or in
9: At that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
The Baptism and Testing of Jesus
10: Just as Jesus was coming up out of the water, he saw heaven being torn open and the Spirit descending on him like a dove.
The Baptism and Testing of Jesus
11: And a voice came from heaven: “You are my Son, whom I love; with you I am well pleased.”
The Baptism and Testing of Jesus
12: At once the Spirit sent him out into the wilderness,
The Baptism and Testing of Jesus
13:
and he was in the wilderness forty days, being tempted
 by Satan. He was with the wild animals, and angels attended him.
Jesus Announces the Good News
1:13 The Greek for tempted can also mean tested.
14: After John was put in prison, Jesus went into Galilee, proclaiming the good news of God.
Jesus Announces the Good News
15: “The time has come,” he said. “The kingdom of God has come near. Repent and believe the good news!”
Jesus Calls His First Disciples
16: As Jesus walked beside the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and his brother Andrew casting a net into the lake, for they were fishermen.
Jesus Calls His First Disciples
17: “Come, follow me,” Jesus said, “and I will send you out to fish for people.”
Jesus Calls His First Disciples
18: At once they left their nets and followed him.
Jesus Calls His First Disciples
19: When he had gone a little farther, he saw James son of Zebedee and his brother John in a boat, preparing their nets.
Jesus Calls His First Disciples
20: Without delay he called them, and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired men and followed him.
Jesus Drives Out an Impure Spirit
21: They went to Capernaum, and when the Sabbath came, Jesus went into the synagogue and began to teach.
Jesus Drives Out an Impure Spirit
22: The people were amazed at his teaching, because he taught them as one who had authority, not as the teachers of the law.
Jesus Drives Out an Impure Spirit
23: Just then a man in their synagogue who was possessed by an impure spirit cried out,
Jesus Drives Out an Impure Spirit
24: “What do you want with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are—the Holy One of God!”
Jesus Drives Out an Impure Spirit
25: “Be quiet!” said Jesus sternly. “Come out of him!”
Jesus Drives Out an Impure Spirit
26: The impure spirit shook the man violently and came out of him with a shriek.
Jesus Drives Out an Impure Spirit
27: The people were all so amazed that they asked each other, “What is this? A new teaching—and with authority! He even gives orders to impure spirits and they obey him.”
Jesus Drives Out an Impure Spirit
28: News about him spread quickly over the whole region of Galilee.
Jesus Heals Many
29: As soon as they left the synagogue, they went with James and John to the home of Simon and Andrew.
Jesus Heals Many
30: Simon’s mother-in-law was in bed with a fever, and they immediately told Jesus about her.
Jesus Heals Many
31: So he went to her, took her hand and helped her up. The fever left her and she began to wait on them.
Jesus Heals Many
32: That evening after sunset the people brought to Jesus all the sick and demon-possessed.
Jesus Heals Many
33: The whole town gathered at the door,
Jesus Heals Many
34: and Jesus healed many who had various diseases. He also drove out many demons, but he would not let the demons speak because they knew who he was.
Jesus Prays in a Solitary Place
35: Very early in the morning, while it was still dark, Jesus got up, left the house and went off to a solitary place, where he prayed.
Jesus Prays in a Solitary Place
36: Simon and his companions went to look for him,
Jesus Prays in a Solitary Place
37: and when they found him, they exclaimed: “Everyone is looking for you!”
Jesus Prays in a Solitary Place
38: Jesus replied, “Let us go somewhere else—to the nearby villages—so I can preach there also. That is why I have come.”
Jesus Prays in a Solitary Place
39: So he traveled throughout Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and driving out demons.
Jesus Heals a Man With Leprosy
40:
A man with leprosy
 came to him and begged him on his knees, “If you are willing, you can make me clean.”
Jesus Heals a Man With Leprosy
1:40 The Greek word traditionally translated leprosy was used for various diseases affecting the skin.
41: Jesus was indignant. He reached out his hand and touched the man. “I am willing,” he said. “Be clean!”
Jesus Heals a Man With Leprosy
1:41 Many manuscripts Jesus was filled with compassion
42: Immediately the leprosy left him and he was cleansed.
Jesus Heals a Man With Leprosy
43: Jesus sent him away at once with a strong warning:
Jesus Heals a Man With Leprosy
44: “See that you don’t tell this to anyone. But go, show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifices that Moses commanded for your cleansing, as a testimony to them.”
Jesus Heals a Man With Leprosy
45: Instead he went out and began to talk freely, spreading the news. As a result, Jesus could no longer enter a town openly but stayed outside in lonely places. Yet the people still came to him from everywhere.
Jesus Heals a Man With Leprosy

1: A few days later, when Jesus again entered Capernaum, the people heard that he had come home.
Jesus Forgives and Heals a Paralyzed Man
2: They gathered in such large numbers that there was no room left, not even outside the door, and he preached the word to them.
Jesus Forgives and Heals a Paralyzed Man
3: Some men came, bringing to him a paralyzed man, carried by four of them.
Jesus Forgives and Heals a Paralyzed Man
4: Since they could not get him to Jesus because of the crowd, they made an opening in the roof above Jesus by digging through it and then lowered the mat the man was lying on.
Jesus Forgives and Heals a Paralyzed Man
5: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralyzed man, “Son, your sins are forgiven.”
Jesus Forgives and Heals a Paralyzed Man
6: Now some teachers of the law were sitting there, thinking to themselves,
Jesus Forgives and Heals a Paralyzed Man
7: “Why does this fellow talk like that? He’s blaspheming! Who can forgive sins but God alone?”
Jesus Forgives and Heals a Paralyzed Man
8: Immediately Jesus knew in his spirit that this was what they were thinking in their hearts, and he said to them, “Why are you thinking these things?
Jesus Forgives and Heals a Paralyzed Man
9: Which is easier: to say to this paralyzed man, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up, take your mat and walk’?
Jesus Forgives and Heals a Paralyzed Man
10: But I want you to know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins.” So he said to the man,
Jesus Forgives and Heals a Paralyzed Man
11: “I tell you, get up, take your mat and go home.”
Jesus Forgives and Heals a Paralyzed Man
12: He got up, took his mat and walked out in full view of them all. This amazed everyone and they praised God, saying, “We have never seen anything like this!”
Jesus Calls Levi and Eats With Sinners
13: Once again Jesus went out beside the lake. A large crowd came to him, and he began to teach them.
Jesus Calls Levi and Eats With Sinners
14: As he walked along, he saw Levi son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax collector’s booth. “Follow me,” Jesus told him, and Levi got up and followed him.
Jesus Calls Levi and Eats With Sinners
15: While Jesus was having dinner at Levi’s house, many tax collectors and sinners were eating with him and his disciples, for there were many who followed him.
Jesus Calls Levi and Eats With Sinners
16: When the teachers of the law who were Pharisees saw him eating with the sinners and tax collectors, they asked his disciples: “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
Jesus Calls Levi and Eats With Sinners
17: On hearing this, Jesus said to them, “It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick. I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners.”
Jesus Questioned About Fasting
18: Now John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting. Some people came and asked Jesus, “How is it that John’s disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees are fasting, but yours are not?”
Jesus Questioned About Fasting
19: Jesus answered, “How can the guests of the bridegroom fast while he is with them? They cannot, so long as they have him with them.
Jesus Questioned About Fasting
20: But the time will come when the bridegroom will be taken from them, and on that day they will fast.
Jesus Questioned About Fasting
21: “No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment. Otherwise, the new piece will pull away from the old, making the tear worse.
Jesus Questioned About Fasting
22: And no one pours new wine into old wineskins. Otherwise, the wine will burst the skins, and both the wine and the wineskins will be ruined. No, they pour new wine into new wineskins.”
Jesus Is Lord of the Sabbath
23: One Sabbath Jesus was going through the grainfields, and as his disciples walked along, they began to pick some heads of grain.
Jesus Is Lord of the Sabbath
24: The Pharisees said to him, “Look, why are they doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath?”
Jesus Is Lord of the Sabbath
25: He answered, “Have you never read what David did when he and his companions were hungry and in need?
Jesus Is Lord of the Sabbath
26: In the days of Abiathar the high priest, he entered the house of God and ate the consecrated bread, which is lawful only for priests to eat. And he also gave some to his companions.”
Jesus Is Lord of the Sabbath
27: Then he said to them, “The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.
Jesus Is Lord of the Sabbath
28: So the Son of Man is Lord even of the Sabbath.”
Jesus Is Lord of the Sabbath

1: Another time Jesus went into the synagogue, and a man with a shriveled hand was there.
Jesus Heals on the Sabbath
2: Some of them were looking for a reason to accuse Jesus, so they watched him closely to see if he would heal him on the Sabbath.
Jesus Heals on the Sabbath
3: Jesus said to the man with the shriveled hand, “Stand up in front of everyone.”
Jesus Heals on the Sabbath
4: Then Jesus asked them, “Which is lawful on the Sabbath: to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill?” But they remained silent.
Jesus Heals on the Sabbath
5: He looked around at them in anger and, deeply distressed at their stubborn hearts, said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out, and his hand was completely restored.
Jesus Heals on the Sabbath
6: Then the Pharisees went out and began to plot with the Herodians how they might kill Jesus.
Crowds Follow Jesus
7: Jesus withdrew with his disciples to the lake, and a large crowd from Galilee followed.
Crowds Follow Jesus
8: When they heard about all he was doing, many people came to him from Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, and the regions across the Jordan and around Tyre and Sidon.
Crowds Follow Jesus
9: Because of the crowd he told his disciples to have a small boat ready for him, to keep the people from crowding him.
Crowds Follow Jesus
10: For he had healed many, so that those with diseases were pushing forward to touch him.
Crowds Follow Jesus
11: Whenever the impure spirits saw him, they fell down before him and cried out, “You are the Son of God.”
Crowds Follow Jesus
12: But he gave them strict orders not to tell others about him.
Jesus Appoints the Twelve
13: Jesus went up on a mountainside and called to him those he wanted, and they came to him.
Jesus Appoints the Twelve
14:
He appointed twelve
 that they might be with him and that he might send them out to preach
Jesus Appoints the Twelve
3:14 Some manuscripts twelve—designating them apostles—
15: and to have authority to drive out demons.
Jesus Appoints the Twelve
16: These are the twelve he appointed: Simon (to whom he gave the name Peter),
Jesus Appoints the Twelve
17:
James son of Zebedee and his brother John (to them he gave the name 
Boanerges
, which means “sons of thunder”),
Jesus Appoints the Twelve
18: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Zealot
Jesus Appoints the Twelve
19: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed him.
Jesus Accused by His Family and by Teachers of the Law
20: Then Jesus entered a house, and again a crowd gathered, so that he and his disciples were not even able to eat.
Jesus Accused by His Family and by Teachers of the Law
21:
When his family
 heard about this, they went to take charge of him, for they said, “He is out of his mind.”
Jesus Accused by His Family and by Teachers of the Law
3:21 Or his associates
22: And the teachers of the law who came down from Jerusalem said, “He is possessed by Beelzebul! By the prince of demons he is driving out demons.”
Jesus Accused by His Family and by Teachers of the Law
23: So Jesus called them over to him and began to speak to them in parables: “How can Satan drive out Satan?
Jesus Accused by His Family and by Teachers of the Law
24: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
Jesus Accused by His Family and by Teachers of the Law
25: If a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
Jesus Accused by His Family and by Teachers of the Law
26: And if Satan opposes himself and is divided, he cannot stand; his end has come.
Jesus Accused by His Family and by Teachers of the Law
27: In fact, no one can enter a strong man’s house without first tying him up. Then he can plunder the strong man’s house.
Jesus Accused by His Family and by Teachers of the Law
28: Truly I tell you, people can be forgiven all their sins and every slander they utter,
Jesus Accused by His Family and by Teachers of the Law
29: but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven; they are guilty of an eternal sin.”
Jesus Accused by His Family and by Teachers of the Law
30: He said this because they were saying, “He has an impure spirit.”
Jesus Accused by His Family and by Teachers of the Law
31: Then Jesus’ mother and brothers arrived. Standing outside, they sent someone in to call him.
Jesus Accused by His Family and by Teachers of the Law
32: A crowd was sitting around him, and they told him, “Your mother and brothers are outside looking for you.”
Jesus Accused by His Family and by Teachers of the Law
33: “Who are my mother and my brothers?” he asked.
Jesus Accused by His Family and by Teachers of the Law
34: Then he looked at those seated in a circle around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers!
Jesus Accused by His Family and by Teachers of the Law
35: Whoever does God’s will is my brother and sister and mother.”
Jesus Accused by His Family and by Teachers of the Law

1: Again Jesus began to teach by the lake. The crowd that gathered around him was so large that he got into a boat and sat in it out on the lake, while all the people were along the shore at the water’s edge.
The Parable of the Sower
2: He taught them many things by parables, and in his teaching said:
The Parable of the Sower
3: “Listen! A farmer went out to sow his seed.
The Parable of the Sower
4: As he was scattering the seed, some fell along the path, and the birds came and ate it up.
The Parable of the Sower
5: Some fell on rocky places, where it did not have much soil. It sprang up quickly, because the soil was shallow.
The Parable of the Sower
6: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched, and they withered because they had no root.
The Parable of the Sower
7: Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up and choked the plants, so that they did not bear grain.
The Parable of the Sower
8: Still other seed fell on good soil. It came up, grew and produced a crop, some multiplying thirty, some sixty, some a hundred times.”
The Parable of the Sower
9: Then Jesus said, “Whoever has ears to hear, let them hear.”
The Parable of the Sower
10: When he was alone, the Twelve and the others around him asked him about the parables.
The Parable of the Sower
11: He told them, “The secret of the kingdom of God has been given to you. But to those on the outside everything is said in parables
The Parable of the Sower
12: so that, “ ‘they may be ever seeing but never perceiving, and ever hearing but never understanding; otherwise they might turn and be forgiven!’ ”
The Parable of the Sower
13: Then Jesus said to them, “Don’t you understand this parable? How then will you understand any parable?
The Parable of the Sower
14: The farmer sows the word.
The Parable of the Sower
15: Some people are like seed along the path, where the word is sown. As soon as they hear it, Satan comes and takes away the word that was sown in them.
The Parable of the Sower
16: Others, like seed sown on rocky places, hear the word and at once receive it with joy.
The Parable of the Sower
17: But since they have no root, they last only a short time. When trouble or persecution comes because of the word, they quickly fall away.
The Parable of the Sower
18: Still others, like seed sown among thorns, hear the word;
The Parable of the Sower
19: but the worries of this life, the deceitfulness of wealth and the desires for other things come in and choke the word, making it unfruitful.
The Parable of the Sower
20: Others, like seed sown on good soil, hear the word, accept it, and produce a crop—some thirty, some sixty, some a hundred times what was sown.”
A Lamp on a Stand
21: He said to them, “Do you bring in a lamp to put it under a bowl or a bed? Instead, don’t you put it on its stand?
A Lamp on a Stand
22: For whatever is hidden is meant to be disclosed, and whatever is concealed is meant to be brought out into the open.
A Lamp on a Stand
23: If anyone has ears to hear, let them hear.”
A Lamp on a Stand
24: “Consider carefully what you hear,” he continued. “With the measure you use, it will be measured to you—and even more.
A Lamp on a Stand
25: Whoever has will be given more; whoever does not have, even what they have will be taken from them.”
The Parable of the Growing Seed
26: He also said, “This is what the kingdom of God is like. A man scatters seed on the ground.
The Parable of the Growing Seed
27: Night and day, whether he sleeps or gets up, the seed sprouts and grows, though he does not know how.
The Parable of the Growing Seed
28: All by itself the soil produces grain—first the stalk, then the head, then the full kernel in the head.
The Parable of the Growing Seed
29: As soon as the grain is ripe, he puts the sickle to it, because the harvest has come.”
The Parable of the Mustard Seed
30: Again he said, “What shall we say the kingdom of God is like, or what parable shall we use to describe it?
The Parable of the Mustard Seed
31: It is like a mustard seed, which is the smallest of all seeds on earth.
The Parable of the Mustard Seed
32: Yet when planted, it grows and becomes the largest of all garden plants, with such big branches that the birds can perch in its shade.”
The Parable of the Mustard Seed
33: With many similar parables Jesus spoke the word to them, as much as they could understand.
The Parable of the Mustard Seed
34: He did not say anything to them without using a parable. But when he was alone with his own disciples, he explained everything.
Jesus Calms the Storm
35: That day when evening came, he said to his disciples, “Let us go over to the other side.”
Jesus Calms the Storm
36: Leaving the crowd behind, they took him along, just as he was, in the boat. There were also other boats with him.
Jesus Calms the Storm
37: A furious squall came up, and the waves broke over the boat, so that it was nearly swamped.
Jesus Calms the Storm
38: Jesus was in the stern, sleeping on a cushion. The disciples woke him and said to him, “Teacher, don’t you care if we drown?”
Jesus Calms the Storm
39: He got up, rebuked the wind and said to the waves, “Quiet! Be still!” Then the wind died down and it was completely calm.
40: He said to his disciples, “Why are you so afraid? Do you still have no faith?”
Jesus Calms the Storm
41: They were terrified and asked each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”

1: They went across the lake to the region of the Gerasenes.
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
5:1 Some manuscripts Gadarenes; other manuscripts Gergesenes
2: When Jesus got out of the boat, a man with an impure spirit came from the tombs to meet him.
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
3: This man lived in the tombs, and no one could bind him anymore, not even with a chain.
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
4: For he had often been chained hand and foot, but he tore the chains apart and broke the irons on his feet. No one was strong enough to subdue him.
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
5: Night and day among the tombs and in the hills he would cry out and cut himself with stones.
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
6: When he saw Jesus from a distance, he ran and fell on his knees in front of him.
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
7: He shouted at the top of his voice, “What do you want with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? In God’s name don’t torture me!”
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
8: For Jesus had said to him, “Come out of this man, you impure spirit!”
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
9: Then Jesus asked him, “What is your name?” “My name is Legion,” he replied, “for we are many.”
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
10: And he begged Jesus again and again not to send them out of the area.
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
11: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the nearby hillside.
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
12: The demons begged Jesus, “Send us among the pigs; allow us to go into them.”
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
13: He gave them permission, and the impure spirits came out and went into the pigs. The herd, about two thousand in number, rushed down the steep bank into the lake and were drowned.
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
14: Those tending the pigs ran off and reported this in the town and countryside, and the people went out to see what had happened.
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
15: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had been possessed by the legion of demons, sitting there, dressed and in his right mind; and they were afraid.
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
16: Those who had seen it told the people what had happened to the demon-possessed man—and told about the pigs as well.
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
17: Then the people began to plead with Jesus to leave their region.
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
18: As Jesus was getting into the boat, the man who had been demon-possessed begged to go with him.
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
19: Jesus did not let him, but said, “Go home to your own people and tell them how much the Lord has done for you, and how he has had mercy on you.”
Jesus Restores a Demon-Possessed Man
20:
So the man went away and began to tell in the Decapolis
 how much Jesus had done for him. And all the people were amazed.
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
5:20 That is, the Ten Cities
21: When Jesus had again crossed over by boat to the other side of the lake, a large crowd gathered around him while he was by the lake.
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
22: Then one of the synagogue leaders, named Jairus, came, and when he saw Jesus, he fell at his feet.
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
23: He pleaded earnestly with him, “My little daughter is dying. Please come and put your hands on her so that she will be healed and live.”
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
24:
So Jesus went with him.
A large crowd followed and pressed around him.
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
25: And a woman was there who had been subject to bleeding for twelve years.
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
26: She had suffered a great deal under the care of many doctors and had spent all she had, yet instead of getting better she grew worse.
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
27: When she heard about Jesus, she came up behind him in the crowd and touched his cloak,
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
28: because she thought, “If I just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
29: Immediately her bleeding stopped and she felt in her body that she was freed from her suffering.
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
30: At once Jesus realized that power had gone out from him. He turned around in the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
31: “You see the people crowding against you,” his disciples answered, “and yet you can ask, ‘Who touched me?’ ”
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
32: But Jesus kept looking around to see who had done it.
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
33: Then the woman, knowing what had happened to her, came and fell at his feet and, trembling with fear, told him the whole truth.
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
34: He said to her, “Daughter, your faith has healed you. Go in peace and be freed from your suffering.”
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
35: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from the house of Jairus, the synagogue leader. “Your daughter is dead,” they said. “Why bother the teacher anymore?”
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
36: Overhearing what they said, Jesus told him, “Don’t be afraid; just believe.”
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
5:36 Or Ignoring
37: He did not let anyone follow him except Peter, James and John the brother of James.
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
38: When they came to the home of the synagogue leader, Jesus saw a commotion, with people crying and wailing loudly.
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
39: He went in and said to them, “Why all this commotion and wailing? The child is not dead but asleep.”
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
40:
But they laughed at him.
After he put them all out, he took the child’s father and mother and the disciples who were with him, and went in where the child was.
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
41: He took her by the hand and said to her, “Talitha koum!” (which means “Little girl, I say to you, get up!” ).
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
42: Immediately the girl stood up and began to walk around (she was twelve years old). At this they were completely astonished.
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman
43: He gave strict orders not to let anyone know about this, and told them to give her something to eat.
Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman

1: Jesus left there and went to his hometown, accompanied by his disciples.
A Prophet Without Honor
2:
When the Sabbath came, he began to teach in the synagogue, and many who heard him were amazed.
“Where did this man get these things?” they asked. “What’s this wisdom that has been given him? What are these remarkable miracles he is performing?
A Prophet Without Honor
3:
Isn’t this the carpenter? Isn’t this Mary’s son and the brother of James, Joseph,
 Judas and Simon? Aren’t his sisters here with us?” And they took offense at him.
A Prophet Without Honor
6:3 Greek Joses, a variant of Joseph
4: Jesus said to them, “A prophet is not without honor except in his own town, among his relatives and in his own home.”
A Prophet Without Honor
5: He could not do any miracles there, except lay his hands on a few sick people and heal them.
A Prophet Without Honor
6:
He was amazed at their lack of faith.
Then Jesus went around teaching from village to village.
Jesus Sends Out the Twelve
7: Calling the Twelve to him, he began to send them out two by two and gave them authority over impure spirits.
Jesus Sends Out the Twelve
8: These were his instructions: “Take nothing for the journey except a staff—no bread, no bag, no money in your belts.
Jesus Sends Out the Twelve
9: Wear sandals but not an extra shirt.
Jesus Sends Out the Twelve

8: Trembling and bewildered, the women went out and fled from the tomb. They said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid. [The earliest manuscripts and some other ancient witnesses do not have verses 9–20.] When Jesus rose early on the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, out of whom he had driven seven demons. She went and told those who had been with him and who were mourning and weeping. When they heard that Jesus was alive and that she had seen him, they did not believe it. Afterward Jesus appeared in a different form to two of them while they were walking in the country. These returned and reported it to the rest; but they did not believe them either. Later Jesus appeared to the Eleven as they were eating; he rebuked them for their lack of faith and their stubborn refusal to believe those who had seen him after he had risen. He said to them, “Go into all the world and preach the gospel to all creation. Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever does not believe will be condemned. And these signs will accompany those who believe: In my name they will drive out demons; they will speak in new tongues; they will pick up snakes with their hands; and when they drink deadly poison, it will not hurt them at all; they will place their hands on sick people, and they will get well.” After the Lord Jesus had spoken to them, he was taken up into heaven and he sat at the right hand of God. Then the disciples went out and preached everywhere, and the Lord worked with them and confirmed his word by the signs that accompanied it.
